title: Access eBooks: UKHSA Knowledge and Library Services
body: "Find eBooks   You can find eBooks by:    using Discovery Search via MyAthens to search for a specific title   browsing the library catalogue  using the resources list in MyAthens to browse the collections detailed below   Publisher collections These can be accessed from the resources list in MyAthens.   Springer Nature eBooks   Includes chapter-level access to tens of thousands of eBooks published by Springer Nature.   This includes the Springer Protocols Handbooks, which provide step-by-step laboratory methods and protocols across the life and biomedical sciences.   Oxford University Press   Includes major titles such as:     Oxford Public Health and Epidemiology  Oxford Textbook of Medicine  Oxford Textbook of Global Health      Taylor and Francis   Offers eBooks on topics including:    data science  statistics  infectious diseases  pollution  health communication    OVID eBooks   Over 270 eBooks are available.  Sage Research Methods (UKHSA staff only)   Provides eBooks covering research methods, statistics, and evaluation.  ScienceDirect Focus Collections (UKHSA staff only) Thousands of eBooks including book series, major reference works and textbooks.   GIDEON guides (UKHSA staff only) The GIDEON eBooks cover infectious diseases and microbiology.   The ‘disease series’ summarises the status of individual or related infectious diseases worldwide, with distribution maps and images.   The ‘country series’ summarises infectious diseases for each country, including vaccination schedules. This includes Infectious Diseases of the World, showing worldwide disease status.   View GIDEON eBooks in the library catalogue.  Subject collections:    agricultural, biological and food sciences  biochemistry, genetics and molecular biology  biomedical science and medicine  environmental sciences  health sciences  immunology and microbiology  pharmacology, toxicology and pharmaceutical sciences   BorrowBox (UKHSA staff only) UKHSA staff can use BorrowBox to browse eBooks, audiobooks, magazines and newspapers. BorrowBox collections include:    wellbeing   learning and development  popular science    You can also access some newspapers and magazines including New Scientist.  Accessing BorrowBox Access UKHSA BorrowBox on your computer or use the BorrowBox app on your mobile phone or tablet.   To read titles on a laptop, install Adobe Digital Editions. Request this from the software self-service catalogue on the IT helpdesk.  Signing in to BorrowBox When using BorrowBox for the first time, enter your institution (UK Health Security Agency). Your login is your library catalogue login (the login you use for the library self-issue kiosks and to view your loans or renew your books online). Your username is the first part of your UKHSA email address before the @ sign. Reset your password if you do not know it.  BorrowBox from multiple libraries If you have BorrowBox access from your local public library, you can still use UKHSA’s BorrowBox collection. Log out of the app and log back in with your UKHSA account to access UKHSA content.  BorrowBox privacy information The BorrowBox Privacy Policy governs the collection, use, and disclosure of personal information by the service.  Feedback This is a new service. Help us improve it by providing feedback.  "
